title: linux: podman-remote silently fails to use RSA ssh keys
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
ssbarnea@leno:~
▶ CONTAINER_HOST=ssh://root@localhost/run/podman/podman.sock podman-remote --log-level=debug ps
INFO[0000] podman-remote filtering at log level debug
DEBU[0000] Called ps.PersistentPreRunE(podman-remote --log-level=debug ps)
DEBU[0000] Found SSH_AUTH_SOCK "/tmp/ssh-IBRTUifY3a/agent.1012473", ssh-agent signer enabled
Error: Failed to create sshClient: Connection to bastion host (ssh://root@localhost/run/podman/podman.sock) failed.: ssh: handshake failed: ssh: unable to authenticate, attempted methods [none publickey], no supported methods remain

# proof that ssh is able to connect correctly (current user has no local keys but is has agent)
ssbarnea@leno:~
▶ ssh root@localhost echo worked!
worked!

▶ ssh-add -l
4096 SHA256:IIgRCJ84QIlEIoYJ1RiFYRwPlbxXVr3z/7jo+FTM6zg /Users/ssbarnea/.ssh/id_rsa (RSA)
```

**Describe the results you received:**

Podman failed to establish ssh remote connection.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Podman should have suceeded establishing connection.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
▶ podman version
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Wed Oct  7 17:21:20 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Yes

comments.body:
@ssbarnea Are you running podman-remote on the same fedora 33 machine as the service?

`DEBU[0000] Found SSH_AUTH_SOCK "/tmp/ssh-IBRTUifY3a/agent.1012473", ssh-agent signer enabled`

should have gotten you in.

For Fedora 33 running the service, I had to add my ed25519.pub key to the authorized_keys file.  When I turned debugging on for sshd I found "ssh-rsa not in PubkeyAcceptedKeyTypes". See https://www.openssh.com/txt/release-8.4

While I already posted this on another macos related ticket, I think it is better suited here:

Confirmed that my both keys are 4096 rsa, one of them is encrypted at rest (loaded into agent) and the other one is unencrypted. podman refuses to connect to the bastion host with both, but ssh has no problems connecting.

The bastion is a fedora 33 machine but i doubt that matters much because ssh works, with RSA keys too!

Now I do have the confirmation that podman is unable to establish ssh connection using RSA keys, regardless if they are loaded by itself or via ssh-agent, it fails to establish a handshake with them:

Error: Failed to create sshClient: Connection to bastion host (ssh://root@leno:22/var/run/podman/podman.sock) failed.: ssh: handshake failed: ssh: unable to authenticate, attempted methods [none publickey], no supported methods remain
Following https://www.ssh.com/ssh/keygen/ I created two other keys ecdsa and ed25519 and they both worked. I skipped testing dsa as I mostly considered unsafe/not-recommended/deprecated.

Basically podman fails to use rsa keys, while ssh does work with them, regardless of what remote server you are trying to connect to. That seams like a bug in ssh implementation used by podman, especially as RSA is still the most commonly used key algorithm.

PS. I also tested with a CentOS 8 host and the behavior was identical, RSA failed silently causing a login prompt, but login prompt is tracked as https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/8498

I have confirmed the same behavior on my Macbook laptop where an ECDSA SSH key works, but RSA does not.

I was using podman-remote 2.1.1 installed via homebrew and a brand new Fedora Silverblue VM that comes default with Fedora Silverblue.

See https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7806#issuecomment-742844281

Executive summary: --identity and ssh-agent work together, --log-level=debug now verbose about key handling to aid debugging, no RSA fix yet
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

AFAIK this bug is still not sorted but it would be useful to see what is causing it. Clearly it has nothing to do with Fedora changer related to RSA because openssh can connect to Fedora boxes using RSA.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Any chance this has been fixed in podman 3.0?

Apparently I had to renable the socker after upgrading to on fedora 33, as the socket was not longer open.  -- `systemctl enable --now podman.socket` 

But after doing this I tested with only one RSA key and it worked, so we can close it as fixed in 3.0 (or 3.0.1 for sure).
Honestly I'm thinking this has more to do with this upstream golang issue: https://github.com/golang/go/issues/37278
